title: Question Words
summary: The question words that let you ask your way through any conversation.

sections.content: Question words are your most powerful tool as a beginner. Even with limited vocabulary, you can point at things, ask what they're called, ask where things are, and ask someone to slow down. Master these and you can navigate almost any situation.

To form a question in Catalan, simply put the question word at the start. The verb order is usually: question word + verb + subject. For example: On és la cuina? (Where is the kitchen?) — on (where) + és (is) + la cuina (the kitchen).

Use 'puc' (can I) and 'pots' (can you) to make polite requests. These come from the verb 'poder' (to be able to). Puc usar el bany? (Can I use the bathroom?) — puc + usar + el bany. Pots repetir? (Can you repeat?) — pots + repetir.

items.catalan: Pots parlar més a poc a poc?

items.note: vol dir = means / wants to say

sections.question: How do you ask 'Can I use the bathroom?'
sections.explanation: 'Puc' means 'can I'. 'On és el bany?' asks where the bathroom is — also useful, but different.

sections.question: Which question word asks 'how much'?
sections.explanation: 'Quant' (m) / 'quanta' (f) = how much. 'Quants/quantes' = how many. Don't confuse with 'quan' = when.

sections.question: How do you ask 'what does this mean?'
sections.explanation: 'Què vol dir...?' literally means 'what wants to say...?' — the standard way to ask for a word's meaning. 'Això' means 'this'.
